title: In Vivo Fiber Photometry Recording With Concurrent Optogenetic Stimulation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dph0u","text":"This protocol describes in vivo fiber photometry recordings from freely moving DAT-Cre mice expressing jGCaMP8f in VTA neurons. Dual-wavelength excitation (470 nm signal channel and 405 nm isosbestic channel) was sinusoidally modulated and demodulated using a Doric fluorescence minicube system. In some experiments, concurrent optogenetic stimulation of Chrimson-expressing neurons was delivered through the same ferrule.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"asyrt","text":"- Avoid spectral overlap in filter configuration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jrlvl","text":"- Use non-opsin controls to confirm absence of optical artifacts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Separation Safeguards**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"th3e3","text":"- Separate excitation and emission bands for GCaMP.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"oteih","text":"- Dedicated return paths for green emission.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fjpqm","text":"- 568 nm channel not modulated at 405 or 470 carrier channel.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Confirm excitation powers at the ferrule tip.
Confirm modulation frequencies remain distinct and stable.
Verify optical power before each session.
Avoid spectral overlap in filter configuration.
Use non-opsin controls to confirm absence of optical artifacts.
creator: Cristian González-Cabrera
